item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents reject the statement from their first turns and remain rights-first through the final turns. Agent A becomes somewhat more forceful by turn 5, and Agent B also closes with a strong rights-as-guardrail frame, but neither visibly reverses or moves toward support. Both concede that public or collective interests can matter under clear, necessary, fair, and proportional limits.
